title: KAPP-Sen TMC: Pancreas Tissue Blocks Paraffin Embedding
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"d0am6","text":"The paraffin cassettes were fixed in 4% paraformaldehyde for 24 hours and processed for paraffin embedding at Joslin Histology Core as described here.\n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":110,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Tissue is processed overnight. Ethanols and Xylenes are at room temperature and paraffin is at 58-60°C Paraffin is Fisherbrand Histoplast PE Cat.#: 22900700
Tissue blocks are transferred to a vacuum paraffin bath.
Cassettes are submersed in 50% EtOH at the end of the day.
Processor programmed to start overnight and finish the following morning.
Tissue blocks are then embedded and placed in 4°C wrapped in foil.
creator: Sergii Domanskyi
